skill.name: reactome-database
skill.description: Query Reactome pathway data and run enrichment analysis through the official Reactome REST APIs and helper script.
skill.summary: Query Reactome pathways, entities, enrichment results, and analysis tokens for systems biology research.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk indicators, but review found most are false positives from markdown REST endpoint examples, HTTP status codes, and documentation text. Confirmed behavior is limited to official Reactome HTTPS requests plus local input-file reads and result-file writes in the helper script. Publishable with a medium-risk warning because it sends user-supplied gene or expression data to an external public API and writes local output files.

medium_findings.title: External Reactome API Submission
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The helper script submits user-provided gene lists to the official Reactome Analysis Service. This is expected for the skill, but it can disclose submitted identifiers or expression-derived data to reactome.org. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: The network destination and POST body are explicit in scripts/reactome_query.py, and the behavior matches the documented skill purpose.

medium_findings.title: Local Result File Write
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The helper script writes full analysis results to a local JSON file derived from the supplied input filename. This is useful behavior, but it can overwrite an existing sibling results file and may store sensitive research data. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The write path is directly derived from the user-provided gene file and opened for writing without an overwrite prompt.

low_findings.title: Documented Network Access to Official Endpoints
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with low security severity. The skill and API reference include many requests examples for reactome.org endpoints. The destinations are hardcoded official Reactome services and no unrelated exfiltration endpoint was found. Confidence: 0.90. Confidence reasoning: All reviewed HTTP examples target reactome.org or reactome.github.io documentation, and the capability is central to the skill.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The many external command findings are caused by markdown fenced examples, REST verbs such as GET and POST, and example shell commands. No subprocess, shell execution, eval, or dynamic command construction was found in the executable helper script. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The flagged SKILL.md and references/api_reference.md lines are documentation examples, and scripts/reactome_query.py contains argparse-style command dispatch without spawning external commands.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Findings Are Semantic False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The weak cryptography, reconnaissance, non-standard device file, and network scanning alerts are triggered by documentation words, HTTP status codes, URLs under /dev, and ordinary biological response fields. No prompt injection, malicious authority claim, weak cryptographic operation, device file access, or network scanning behavior was found. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: Targeted review of the flagged areas showed Reactome API documentation and examples rather than operational security-sensitive code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network POST of User-Supplied Analysis Data
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill posts user-provided identifiers to the Reactome Analysis Service. This is expected functionality, but users should treat submitted gene lists and expression data as data shared with an external service. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: The POST target and submitted data are directly visible in the helper script and documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local JSON Output Write
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script writes full Reactome analysis results to a local JSON output file. This is not malicious, but it creates local data persistence and possible overwrite behavior. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The file write is explicit and uses a filename derived from the user-supplied input path.

content.value_statement: Pathway research often requires repeated API queries and careful result interpretation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Reactome pathway lookup, enrichment analysis, token retrieval, and result review.
actual_capabilities: Query Reactome Content Service records by stable pathway or entity identifier.
Submit gene or protein lists to the Reactome Analysis Service for enrichment analysis.
Retrieve previous analysis results with Reactome analysis tokens while they remain valid.
Map participating physical entities for a pathway or reaction.
Generate Reactome Pathway Browser links for analyzed pathways.
Use the included Python helper script for version, query, search, entity, and analysis commands.
limitations: Requires network access to reactome.org for live API queries.
Does not validate biological conclusions beyond Reactome API response data.
Analysis tokens are controlled by Reactome and are valid for a limited time.
The helper script writes result files locally when running enrichment analysis.

use_cases.title: Gene List Enrichment Review
use_cases.description: Submit a researcher gene list, identify enriched Reactome pathways, and summarize significant FDR results.

use_cases.title: Pathway Entity Lookup
use_cases.description: Retrieve pathway details, reactions, proteins, complexes, and participating entities for a known Reactome stable ID.

use_cases.title: Workflow Scripting Support
use_cases.description: Use the included helper script to automate common Reactome queries from a terminal workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Look Up a Pathway
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Reactome skill to look up pathway R-HSA-69278. Summarize the pathway name, species, type, and key participating entities.

prompt_templates.title: Run Enrichment Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this gene list with Reactome overrepresentation analysis: TP53, BRCA1, EGFR, MYC, CDK1. Report the top pathways and FDR values.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Analysis Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve Reactome analysis results for this token and compare the top significant pathways by found entities, p-value, and FDR.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Research Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a reproducible Reactome workflow for a gene expression study. Include input formatting, enrichment submission, result filtering, browser links, and local output handling.

output_examples.output: A concise pathway summary with the Reactome stable ID, display name, species, schema class, and a short list of participating entities.

output_examples.input: Analyze TP53, BRCA1, EGFR, MYC, and CDK1.
output_examples.output: A ranked pathway report showing enriched pathway names, matched entity counts, p-values, FDR values, and a Reactome browser link.

output_examples.input: Prepare my expression table for Reactome analysis.
output_examples.output: A format checklist explaining the required header row, identifier column, numeric sample columns, and submission approach.

best_practices: Confirm gene identifiers before submission to reduce unmapped entities.
Use batch analysis for gene lists instead of many individual pathway requests.
Review FDR values and pathway context before making biological conclusions.
anti_patterns: Do not treat Reactome enrichment as proof of causation.
Do not submit sensitive unpublished datasets without checking data sharing rules.
Do not rely on expired analysis tokens for reproducible research records.

faq.question: Does this skill require internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. Live queries use the official Reactome Content Service and Analysis Service endpoints.

faq.question: Can it analyze expression data?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents Reactome expression analysis input rules and submission through the Analysis Service.

faq.question: Does it include a script?
faq.answer: Yes. scripts/reactome_query.py provides command-line helpers for version, query, search, entities, and analyze commands.

faq.question: Where are enrichment results stored?
faq.answer: The helper script writes a local results file next to the input gene list when analysis is run.

faq.question: Can it retrieve old results?
faq.answer: Yes, if the Reactome analysis token is still valid. The skill states tokens are valid for seven days.

faq.question: Is reactome2py required?
faq.answer: No. The skill supports direct REST API examples and notes that reactome2py is optional and not actively maintained.
